description:
In quickheadlines.cr WebSocket handler, if host header is nil or empty, the origin validation logic will fail silently and skip validation. While the initial check for missing origin in non-development mode is good, if origin IS provided but host is nil, we skip the cross-origin check.

**Location:** src/quickheadlines.cr

**Code path:**
Usage: crystal [command] [switches] [program file] [--] [arguments]

Command:
    init                     generate a new project
    build                    build an executable
    clear_cache              clear the compiler cache
    docs                     generate documentation
    env                      print Crystal environment information
    eval                     eval code from args or standard input
    i/interactive            starts interactive Crystal
    play                     starts Crystal playground server
    run (default)            build and run program
    spec                     build and run specs (in spec directory)
    tool                     run a tool
    help, --help, -h         show this help
    version, --version, -v   show version

Run a command followed by --help to see command specific information, ex:
    crystal <command> --help

**Security issue:** An attacker could send:
- Origin: https://evil.com
- Host: (nil or empty)

This would pass the first check (!origin && !is_development) since origin exists, then skip the detailed validation since host is nil.

**Fix:** If origin is provided, host must also be provided. Return 400 Bad Request if origin exists but host doesn't.
title: Bug: WebSocket Origin check - potential bypass via null host header
